data.epi_content:
"I didn't want to fight with Evan."

What's different from before?

They could have at least sent me back to an earlier time.

For example, before Vivian took my hand, or even yesterday.

Well, I guess I should be grateful there's plenty of coffee.

I don't even need to buy it separately; just pull the trigger once and coffee is provided infinitely.

Talk about economic innovation.

To be honest, I don't want to do that again.

It hurt too much.

And more than the physical pain, I hate that miserable feeling.

I tried hard to convince myself with various empty, hollow reasons why I should give up on life, but when I actually stabbed my arm, I realized this wasn't right.

So instead of finding life as a second-best option, I looked for a gun that might hurt less or end the pain instantly.

When it comes to killing people, firearms have always been perfect.

And even then, I realized that if you're stupid, you can't use them properly.

Well, I guess I succeeded in dying in the end.

They say before you die, you see your life flash before your eyes and want to live at the last moment, but more precisely, I didn't just want to cling to life—I wanted to live properly and happily.

What's the point of surviving a suicide attempt if I'm certain the same world that made me want to die will unfold before me again?

There's a fool in Greek mythology called Sisyphus.

He angered the arrogant gods and was punished to push a boulder up a mountain.

When he reaches the top, the boulder rolls back down, and he has to push it up again.

No matter how hard he tries, it repeats endlessly.

That life of endless repetition, without end or hope.

And after giving up on that repetitive life, I was punished by someone nameless.

An indescribable punishment where I have to devote everything to something I can never accomplish.

Trying to escape reality, I speak out loud.

"So now I just go to class?

Lydia will start laying the groundwork to torment me.

Then after being bullied once, I'll lock myself in my room and the cycle repeats."

But after dying, I suddenly realized something.

I must really hate being alone.

I must really hate being lonely, solitary, with no one caring about me.

So when Vivian came looking for me, even though I denied it, I was actually happy.

No one cared about me.

Even Evan, though he had his reasons, didn't come looking for me.

But Vivian did.

"Hmph."

It was a bit absurd.

If I hadn't entered this little lady's body in the first place, what would have happened by now?

It would have just proven that there was once a human named Erica, and all she left in the world was a bit of darkness in Evan and Vivian's hearts, nothing more.

And this mess wouldn't have happened, and the world would have moved on with Vivian's love story.

I keep drinking coffee.

When I need to use the bathroom, I go briefly, then return to drinking coffee endlessly.

My eyes feel slightly dizzy, but I like how my mind becomes hazy.

And before I knew it, morning arrived.

I roughly brush my messy hair, grab a book, and head to the classroom.

I open the door, put on my shoes, go into the hallway, down the stairs, and toward the classroom.

The world that once looked like a dreary monotone now seems to have some color.
data.epi_content2:
No matter how hard life is, isn't this why humans seek comfort items?

Even when you're dying from hardship, sitting in a comfortable chair drinking coffee and eating your favorite snack relieves at least a tiny bit of stress.

As I enter the classroom, Lydia approaches me with a friendly smile.

"Good morning, Erica!"

"...Yes, good morning."

"By the way, have you heard the news?"

If I ask what news, she'll say, "The Duke had an accident!" and start slowly feeding on me.

"Yes, I've heard."

"Hmm, is that so?

Anyway, miss, you know I've never truly respected you from the bottom of my heart, right?"

While I grab a desk, pull out a chair, and place a book on it to settle in, Lydia follows me around, dropping words here and there.

"I know."

"Then you must also know that after the Duke passes away, when all your backing collapses, I won't respect you anymore?"

Hearing this, I was momentarily stunned when Lydia tossed a piece of paper trash at me.

It's the childish way of bullying kids you don't like during class—invisibly, in a childish manner, petty and annoying.

And as you gradually tolerate these things, the level of bullying escalates.

Anyway, though it seems childish, it's quite effective.

It's a low-level prank that's hard to complain about.

"...I suppose so."

"Why are you taking this so calmly? Perhaps you're not really his child..."

Slap.

The sound of a palm hitting a cheek rings out.

Simultaneously, Lydia's face turns.

"How about you shut your mouth? The stench of rot is overwhelming."

"Ha, very well. I'll listen.

It seems you haven't grasped reality yet, so I'll listen."

Reality check? I understand it all too well.

That's probably why I'm acting this way.

If I show weakness, she'll pretend to comfort me while actually tearing into me, and those wounds will hurt too much to heal for a long time.

I thought I was betrayed back then, but thinking about it again, you were just continuing what you'd always done.

You just didn't do things against my wishes until the person above me disappeared, and then you started running wild.

While trampling your disliked former master in the process.

"You mean after my father passes away, the family will fall apart with no one to manage it, internal conflict will break out, and it will either fragment or be taken by the imperial family?"

At my words, Lydia's expression sours, cracking her smiling face.

"For someone who knows this, is it okay to act like this?"

"To quote you, 'very well.' Nothing will change anyway."

"Do as you please, miss.

Our miss, you don't have to pretend to be so strong, haha."

"Am I pretending?"
data.epi_content3:
"Yes."

Lydia naturally interprets my actions as pretending to be something I'm not.

But simply put.

I'm just squirming after being stepped on.

After class begins, attendance is called, and as always, only the attentive students listen carefully.

I wasn't particularly interested even though time was repeating.

I didn't think I needed classes about how great historical figures were when in this world, you only needed to know your family's own great figures.

After class ends, since Lydia probably won't bother me today, I take the money I brought and head to the restaurant district.

Well, even if all the students are brought there, they're nobles with quite generous spending habits.

Even places that provide free food serve decent meals, but most consider going there somewhat shameful.

I planned to eat there when my money ran out.

Though I never went because Lydia sent kids to catch me and prevent me from eating.

Anyway, that's why there's a street full of high-end restaurants within the Academy grounds.

The children's vanity stemming from their status, and parents who consider that vanity natural, created demand, and where there's demand, supply follows.

Coming out to the restaurant, I saw familiar faces.

The Crown Prince I'd glimpsed once before, Vivian with a slight red mark on her cheek, Evan and a taciturn-looking person, and one unknown woman.

Then Vivian, looking back, met my eyes.

I averted my gaze to pretend I didn't see her and immediately turned around.

After what happened yesterday, she wouldn't come looking for me with good intentions.

I hope not, at least.

Fortunately, it seems only Vivian saw me, so there shouldn't be any problem.

I keep running away.

In the end, after running and running and running again, I ended up with that ugly death in the bathtub.

Now even my final choice has been taken away.

What should I do?

What am I supposed to do now?

What will happen to me?

Why, why did it have to be me?

Shaking these complicated thoughts from my head, I enter any restaurant.

As soon as I enter, I smell meat.

"...Ugh."

Feeling nauseous, I turn around and flee back to my room.

As always.

Just like before I became this young lady.

I brew a cup of coffee and put a chocolate in my mouth.

Eating something can at least satisfy a hungry stomach.

"This is bitter."

The chocolate feels much more bitter than the coffee that at least has some aroma.

After chewing for a while, I just spit it out.

The floor will probably be cleaned by a maid who will come someday.

They should have added more sugar.

At least it's not red, so that's good.
